hawkish_phrases: gap appeared to be emerging between favourable economic conditions and a policy stance that remained in a crisis configuration
an unchanged monetary policy setting would become increasingly expansionary
further easing of financial conditions was not regarded as warranted
communication needed to evolve gradually in step with improving economic data
the relative importance of the forward guidance on policy rates would increase
dovish_phrases: underlying price pressures remained muted overall
measures of underlying inflation had retreated slightly
convergence remained contingent on continued support provided by the full range of the ECB's monetary policy measures
caution remained warranted and that the projected upward path for inflation was subject to considerable uncertainty
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged and confirmed the October 2017 APP recalibration (net purchases at €60bn until December 2017, then €30bn from January 2018), with unanimous agreement to maintain all forward guidance despite significantly upgraded growth projections, emphasizing increased confidence in inflation convergence while stressing that underlying inflation remained muted and ample accommodation was still necessary.
